Antam Gold Extends Decline, Slips to Rp 2.98 Mill as Liquidity Demand Caps Safe-Haven Rally
body: Antam gold fell to Rp 2.988 million as liquidity demand capped gains, despite a 20% surge so far in 2026.

title: JCI Rebounds 0.75%  as Bargain Hunting Kicks In Despite Global Risks
body: JCI rebounds above 7,000, rising 0.75% as bargain hunting emerges despite global risks and Fed uncertainty.

title: 'We Are Not A Colonialist': Indonesia on US Forced Labor Probe
body: Indonesia has denied forced labor allegations as the US launched an unfair trade probe into Southeast Asia’s biggest economy.

title: Indonesia Keeps $19.8 Billion Meal Program Untouched in Budget Austerity
body: Indonesia wants to cut wasteful spending related to the free meal rollout as Jakarta prepares to offset the impact of the Iran war.

title: Finance Minister Dismisses Recession Fears as Consumption Remains Strong
body: Indonesia rejects recession fears as officials point to strong growth, rising consumer spending and improving economic indicators.

title: Indonesia Prepares Contingency Plan if High Oil Prices Persist
body: Indonesia may cut spending and revise its deficit cap if oil prices remain high for long, though current fiscal pressures remain manageable.

title: Indonesia Says It's Still in Safe Zone, No Need to Lift 3% Deficit Cap
body: Indonesia once lifted the 3% deficit cap when the Covid-19 pandemic hit the country.

title: Prabowo: Indonesia Must Not Spend Beyond Its Means
body: President says deficit rule remains key fiscal discipline tool as budget gap reaches Rp 135.7 trillion by February.

title: Oil Shock Pushes Rupiah to Brink of Rp 17,000 as Middle East War Escalates
body: The rupiah hovered near Rp 17,000 as oil jumped toward $100 after tensions around the Strait of Hormuz rattled markets.

title: JCI Extends Losses, Tumbles 1.61% as Middle East War Drives Oil Above $100
body: JCI sank 1.61% as Middle East tensions pushed oil above $100, raising inflation fears and adding pressure to Indonesia’s fiscal outlook.

title: OJK Warns Rising Global Risks Could Push Up Indonesian Banks’ Funding Costs
body: OJK warns rising global risks and sovereign outlook changes may increase funding costs for Indonesian banks.

title: Indonesia Plans Tighter E-Commerce Rules to Curb Online Shopping Scams
body: Indonesia plans to strengthen e-commerce regulations after Rp 18.2 billion in consumer complaints linked to scams and disputes in 2025.

title: Government Borrowing Lifts Indonesia External Debt to $434.7 B, Bank Indonesia Reports
body: Indonesia’s external debt reached $434.7B in Jan. as government borrowing increased while private-sector external debt continued to decline.

title: Trade Minister Says Food Prices Stable Ahead of Eid as Government Steps Up Market Monitoring
body: Government says staple food prices remain stable and supplies sufficient ahead of Eid.

title: Antam Gold Extends Decline as Global Prices Slip on Inflation Fears and Fed Rate Outlook
body: Antam gold falls Rp 5,000 to Rp 2.992M per gram as rising oil prices fuel inflation worries and cloud Fed rate-cut outlook.

title: Indonesia Stocks Start Week in Red as Oil Tops $100 and Global Risk-Off Sentiment Builds
body: JCI opens 0.3% lower to 7,115 as oil rallies above $100, Hormuz tensions escalate and investors turn cautious before BI’s policy meeting.

title: Nearly Half of Economists Say Indonesia’s Economy Is Deteriorating: UI Survey
body: A survey shows most economists see Indonesia’s economy stagnating or worsening as inflation, weak jobs, and policy concerns weigh on growth.

title: Three Oil Scenarios Show Indonesia Deficit Breaching 3% Limit
body: Airlangga says all oil price scenarios threaten to push Indonesia’s budget deficit above the 3% legal cap, testing fiscal discipline.

title: China’s Weiming Now Runs Two Indonesian Waste-to-Energy Projects
body: The latest announcement further cements Chinese dominance in Indonesia's multibillion-dollar plan to turn waste into watts.

title: IDX Market Cap Loses $56 Billion in Oil-Driven Stock Selloff
body: Indonesia stocks fell 5.91% in a week as rising oil prices and a holiday trading lull weighed on sentiment and market activity.
